summary: Alfred demonstrates strong error handling discipline throughout its production code. A typed AlfredError enum with thiserror is used for file I/O, a LispError enum covers parse and runtime failures, and PluginError covers plugin lifecycle failures. All fallible I/O operations at module boundaries return Result<T, E>. Command and hook failures are surfaced to the user as status messages rather than crashes. The main gaps are: the undo_stack has no size cap (unbounded memory accumulation on long editing sessions), a number of .unwrap() calls exist in production paths that are logically safe but not proven safe to the compiler, and syntax highlighting query compilation silently discards errors via .ok().

swallowed_exceptions.context: Query::new(&config.grammar, config.highlight_query).ok() -- query compilation errors are silently dropped, resulting in no syntax highlighting with no diagnostic
fs::read_dir error on the plugins directory returns (vec![], vec![]) with no error -- callers cannot distinguish 'no plugins' from 'directory unreadable'
Individual DirEntry errors during list-dir iteration are silently skipped with `Err(_) => continue` -- no logging or partial error reporting

missing_error_handling.description: push_undo() pushes to undo_stack without any size limit. On a long editing session with large files, this will cause unbounded memory growth. The change_list and jump_list have MAX_SIZE limits but undo_stack does not.
parser.set_language() error is caught (returns false) but the callers in the event loop do not check the return value; highlighting silently degrades without a user-visible message.

Plugin discovery errors are collected and displayed as a single joined string. If many plugins fail, the full error list is concatenated and may exceed the visible message area; individual plugin errors are not individually actionable.

locations: crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs:1505 -- ct_event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) prevents indefinite terminal event blocking
crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs:1449 -- crossterm::terminal::size() falls back to (80, 24) via unwrap_or
crates/alfred-plugin/src/discovery.rs:16 -- missing plugins dir returns empty results instead of crashing
crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs:1581 -- unknown commands display a message rather than panicking

layer_analysis.pattern: Result<T, AlfredError> for all fallible I/O; pure functions return values directly
RuntimeError returned from all native closures; LispError wraps parse/runtime/io failures
PluginError with named variants; errors collected rather than short-circuited during scan
io::Result<()> at the top level; Lisp/command errors become editor messages instead of propagating
Box<dyn Error> at main boundary; plugin errors downgraded to message strings

cross_boundary_leaks.description: Internal Lisp RuntimeError messages (potentially containing symbol names and expression trees) are exposed verbatim in the editor status line

logging_assessment.notes: No structured logging framework is used. Errors are surfaced to the user via the editor message line rather than log files. There is no eprintln/log crate usage in production paths except for the fatal error path in main.rs. This is acceptable for a local editor but makes debugging plugin issues difficult.

recommendations.title: Cap the undo_stack size to prevent unbounded memory growth
recommendations.description: Add a UNDO_MAX_SIZE constant (e.g., 1000) to push_undo() and drain oldest entries when the limit is exceeded, mirroring the existing pattern used for jump_list and change_list. Since Rope clones are O(1) via structural sharing, individual snapshots are cheap but the stack is unbounded.

recommendations.title: Propagate query compilation errors as editor messages
recommendations.description: In SyntaxHighlighter::set_language_for_file(), replace `.ok()` on Query::new() with explicit error handling that sets a diagnostic on the highlighter, and surface the message in the event loop so the user can see when highlighting fails to initialize.

recommendations.title: Add structured logging for plugin and Lisp errors
recommendations.description: Introduce the `log` crate (with `env_logger` or `tracing`) to write plugin load failures and hook errors to a log file (e.g., ~/.local/share/alfred/alfred.log). This would allow post-hoc debugging of plugin issues without requiring the user to reproduce interactively.
